source_jobs.title: Director of Retention
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for Lineage Provisions, A real-food nutrition company focused on regeneratively raised meat snacks and supplements, crafted to make eating like a human easy, honest, and impactful.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We’re hiring a Director of Retention to own the full customer lifecycle, from first purchase through long-term loyalty, across email, SMS, subscriptions, direct mail, post-purchase flows, winbacks, LTV cohorts, landing page optimization with tight messaging, and beyond.

Lineage is just over two years old, yet our owned channels are scaling at a rate rarely seen in CPG:

 - $25M+ in revenue driven from email + SMS

 - Nearly $2M in MRR from subscriptions, launched less than a year ago

 - Blended LTV (including one time buyers) $154 with LTV:CAC 3:1

We are only getting started.

Your mandate: Grow and deepen the value of our customer base and all of the new customers we are acquiring. That means building an email list of 1 million engaged real-food subscribers while driving measurable lifts in retention rates, increase in subscribers, and LTV. You’ll scale our subscription program into one of the strongest in the category, transform post-purchase into a loyalty engine, and turn first-time buyers into long-term Lineage advocates.

This role is both strategic and operational. You will work closely with our Director of Marketing and VP of Growth to develop the lifecycle roadmap, performance targets, campaign calendar, and experimentation plan for retention.

At the same time, you're not afraid to get hands-on: building tests, constructing segmentation logic, optimizing flows, pulling data, QA’ing campaigns, and jumping in alongside a designer, and email coordinator or executive assistant whenever needed, but you can build and ship yourself with prolific speed using AI.

You should live and breathe Klaviyo, understand retention levers like LTV, repeat rate by customer type and product, churn signals, LTV:CAC, and cohort behavior, and thrive in an environment where we create hypotheses, test them, review learnings, and operationalize quickly.





RESPONSIBILITIES

Email, SMS, Direct Mail

 - Fully own Klaviyo—segmentation, flows, deliverability, testing, performance reporting, optimization.

 - Build and manage email + SMS campaign and flow calendars across product launches, educational campaigns, promos, and retention themes, sending 20-30 campaigns a month.

 - Oversee voice memo SMS campaigns from founders and consistently test new formats that strengthen connection and loyalty.

 - Partner with contractors to brief, load, QA, and deploy campaigns and be able to use AI tooling to 10x this with automated systems.

 - Coordinate strategy and design for direct mail campaigns.

 - Ensure a high bar for brand alignment, clarity, performance, and customer relevance.

Subscription Strategy & Growth

 - Own subscription retention and growth: pricing strategy, churn mitigation, personalization, win backs, plan upgrades, save strategies, and promotion tactics and manage everything in Skio.

 - Drive sub attach rate without using scammy gray hat tactics for both new customer and one time to sub, dependent on product and acquisition source.

 - You are obsessed with NRR and are always looking at this number, trying to drive over 100%.

 - Work closely with the Director of Marketing to refine subscription offerings, test new retention mechanisms, and optimize onboarding.

 - Use cohort analysis, predictive modeling, and customer behavior data to build long-term strategies on increasing LTV across segments.

Customer Lifecycle Architecture

 - Architect personalized journeys across channels: onboarding, activation, loyalty, replenishment, reactivation, and advocacy.

 - Obsess over first order to repeat purchase data, time to second purchase, and strategies to improve these metrics to best in class retention by cohort.

 -

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client advancing the future of personalized health through clinical-grade microbiome testing—helping individuals and practitioners uncover root causes and take a more proactive, data-driven approach to gut and vaginal health.




ABOUT THE COMPANY

We’re a fast-growing precision health company transforming how people understand and improve their health through advanced microbiome science.

Founded by scientists and physicians, the platform uses clinical-grade testing and genomic technology to deliver personalized, actionable insights for both consumers and practitioners—making preventive, data-driven care more accessible.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a highly organized, relationship-driven, and proactive Customer Success Manager to own and grow B2B partnership accounts.

This role will serve as the primary point of contact for partners post-sale—leading onboarding, driving adoption, and ensuring long-term retention and expansion. You’ll work cross-functionally with Product, Operations, Marketing, and Clinical teams while building scalable systems and playbooks to support a high-quality partner experience.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


CUSTOMER LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT

 - Own the full partner lifecycle: onboarding, adoption, engagement, renewal, and expansion

 - Serve as the primary point of contact and trusted advisor for B2B partners

 - Lead regular check-ins and quarterly business reviews (QBRs) to align on goals, KPIs, and value delivered


ACCOUNT HEALTH & RETENTION

 - Develop and monitor customer health metrics; proactively identify and mitigate risk

 - Drive retention and expansion through strong relationship management and performance insights

 - Track account performance and communicate key trends and opportunities to leadership


OPERATIONAL EXECUTION & PROCESS BUILDING

 - Manage and provide oversight to an offshore resource supporting partner operations and customer support

 - Maintain clean account documentation, success plans, and internal trackers

 - Build scalable playbooks, templates, and processes to improve efficiency and consistency


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with Product, Operations, Marketing, and Clinical teams to resolve issues and improve the customer experience

 - Share customer feedback internally and advocate for improvements that drive retention and growth

 - Support development of partner-facing enablement and marketing resources (e.g., onboarding materials, training decks, case studies)




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Strong partner retention and growth across accounts

 - High partner satisfaction and engagement

 - Reduced churn and faster resolution of issues and escalations

 - Scalable systems and processes that improve the overall partner experience




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–5+ years in Customer Success, Account Management, or a related role

 - Experience managing B2B accounts with structured QBRs and executive stakeholders

 - Strong analytical skills with experience using CRMs and reporting tools (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce, Looker, Zoho)

 - Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

 - Highly organized with strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, ambiguous startup environment




KEY COMPETENCIES

 - High EQ relationship builder with strong communication skills

 - Strategic thinker and problem solver

 - Strong cross-functional collaborator and influencer

 - Process-oriented with a systems mindset

 - Proactive, detail-oriented, and resourceful




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in healthtech, diagnostics, biotech, or wellness

 - Experience building customer health scoring models or dashboards

 - Familiarity with content creation tools (e.g., Canva, Google Slides) for partner-facing materials

 - Startup experience or comfort wearing m

source_jobs.title: Head of Content & Brand
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We're hiring a Head of Content & Brand to lead the content and brand engine behind Paul Saladino, MD.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We're hiring a Head of Content & Brand to lead our content and brand engine. This is a senior leadership role: you own content and brand strategy across the ecosystem, set the creative and editorial standard, and lead the channel-specific resources that bring it to life — our short-form team and our YouTube / long-form team. You are accountable for what we publish, how consistently we publish it, and how it performs.

You are not the person posting every caption — you are the person who builds the system, leads the people inside it, and protects the brand. Equal parts strategist, operator, and editor-in-chief.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own content and brand strategy across short-form, long-form, and emerging channels

 - Lead and manage the channel-specific resources — the short-form team and the YouTube / long-form team

 - Be the guardian of our voice, tone, and brand standard across everything we publish

 - Translate our ideas and the brand's mission into a clear, prioritized content roadmap

 - Build and own the systems, calendars, and workflows that keep the engine running without "push"

 - Own performance, growth, and reporting — and turn insights into the next content bets

 - Develop the team: set expectations, give direct feedback, and raise the bar on quality




QUALIFICATIONS

 - You've led content or creative teams and owned outcomes, not just tasks for a personal brand

 - You think in systems and strategy — clarity and structure excite you

 - You've managed content calendars, editors, and publishing pipelines at scale

 - Strong editorial and creative judgment — you know what makes content work

 - Strong writing fundamentals, especially concise, punchy hooks and captions

 - Analytical — you track, measure, and optimize output against goals

 - Comfortable giving and receiving clear, direct feedback as a leader

 - Thrive in a fast-moving environment with shifting priorities

 - Personal alignment with animal-based / functional health principles

source_jobs.title: Head of US Sales - Supplements
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client, a biotechnology and health company developing anti-PFAS probiotic solutions designed to help remove persistent environmental toxins from the body and support long-term gut and metabolic health.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We are building the category leader in anti-PFAS gut health. Our flagship product, is a probiotic designed to help remove PFAS and other persistent toxins from the body—an issue increasingly recognized across health, longevity, and occupational exposure communities.

We are seeking a Sales Leader to lead commercial expansion in the United States and broader Americas region. This role will own customer acquisition, partnerships, and distribution strategy.

This is a highly entrepreneurial role for someone who thrives in early-stage growth environments. You will be responsible for building the initial sales engine—developing market penetration strategies, establishing distribution partnerships, and personally driving customer acquisition in key segments.

Success in this role means establishing us as the leading anti-PFAS supplement brand in the U.S., building a strong portfolio of customers and partners, and laying the commercial foundation for future growth.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Commercial Strategy & Market Penetration

 - Lead commercial expansion in the United States

 - Develop and execute structured market penetration plans targeting priority customer segments.

 - Identify and prioritize beachhead segments including firefighters, biohackers, and longevity-focused consumers.

Sales & Customer Acquisition

 - Drive direct customer acquisition through field sales, partnerships, and distribution relationships.

 - Build, maintain, and grow a portfolio of key customers and commercial partners.

 - Maintain daily dialogue with new and existing customers to strengthen relationships and drive revenue.

Partnerships & Distribution

 - Identify and negotiate partnerships with distributors and strategic partners.

 - Develop relationships with relevant organizations and service providers (e.g., diagnostics partners).

Operational Leadership

 - Own CRM management and reporting (HubSpot currently implemented).

 - Track sales performance, pipeline development, and commercial progress.

 - Hire and manage initial commercial team members as the organization scales.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 6+ years of experience in dietary supplements, functional health, longevity, or adjacent consumer health categories.

 - Strong understanding of health, wellness, biohacking, or longevity markets.

 - Demonstrated success in direct sales, partnerships, or distribution development.

 - Experience building and managing customer portfolios and long-term relationships.

 - Ability to develop structured commercial strategies and execute them independently.

 - Experience with CRM systems (HubSpot preferred).

 - Comfortable operating in a hands-on role that includes field sales and customer acquisition.

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a leading consumer Health & Wellness Platform — that has existing partnerships with Eight Sleep, WHOOP, Momentous, Peloton, and other top brands.




JOB DESCRIPTION

Our GTM and Operations teams rely on rigorous, timely analysis to move fast and make confident decisions — this role is the engine behind that capability. You sit at the intersection of data and decision-making, transforming raw inputs into the dashboards, models, and analyses that keep the business moving.

By driving KPI clarity and enabling self-serve analytics, you reduce friction across the organization and free teams to act on insight rather than hunt for it.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain dashboards that give leadership a real-time view of the business: revenue, merchant performance, transaction volume, and customer metrics.

 - Deliver ad hoc analyses for GTM and Operations teams, translating business questions into clear findings and recommendations.

 - Design and maintain datasets and models that empower business teams to self-serve.

 - Use AI tools to move faster, automate repetitive analysis, and focus time on the work that drives the most impact.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–5+ years of experience in an analytics-focused role.

 - Strong SQL skills: you can independently query, validate, and explore data without engineer support.

 - Strong proficiency with at least one BI tool — Omni, Looker, Metabase, Tableau, or similar — and a track record of building dashboards from scratch that teams use to drive decisions.

 - Familiarity with dbt and modern data stack concepts sufficient to collaborate with data and engineering teams.

source_jobs.title: Customer Experience Lead
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a leading consumer Health & Wellness Platform — that has existing partnerships with Eight Sleep, WHOOP, Momentous, Peloton, and other top brands.




JOB DESCRIPTION

As Customer Experience Lead, you'll own the day-to-day support experience across written channels while building the systems, workflows, and team capacity that allow us to scale without sacrificing quality.

This is a hands-on role: you'll spend meaningful time directly supporting customers while simultaneously leading continuous improvement across tooling, documentation, and team performance. You'll combine high-quality written communication with operational rigor — shipping SOPs, improving AI workflows, and turning customer feedback into clear, actionable product insights.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the customer support experience across written channels (email and/or messaging).

 - Manage and coach a small team of contractors — set clear expectations, deliver training, and provide ongoing feedback.

 - Drive operational excellence: deploy and improve AI workflows, conduct QA, and build macros, templates, and documentation that help agents support customers consistently and efficiently.

 - Identify recurring customer issues and partner with Product, Engineering, and Operations to resolve root causes.

 - Evolve support tooling — including AI automation — to improve speed, accuracy, and consistency across all agents.

 - Build scalable knowledge resources, including internal SOPs and customer-facing help content, that reduce inbound support volume over time.

 - Track and report on support health metrics: CSAT, volume, response times, resolution quality, and customer reviews.

 - Own customer escalations as needed; partner with Merchant Success and Product teams to ensure customers receive clear, empathetic, and successful outcomes.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3–5 years of experience in customer support or customer experience, including direct ownership of common support metrics.

 - Strong written communication skills.

 - Understanding of core support metrics (CSAT, response time, resolution rate) and demonstrated ability to influence them.

 - Track record of improving processes, not just executing them.

 - Ability to analyze trends and communicate insights clearly to cross-functional partners.

 - Experience managing or mentoring others.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Associate
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client developing clinical-grade metabolic assessment technology used by health, fitness, medical, and academic professionals to deliver more precise, data-driven training and nutrition protocols. Their platform sits at the intersection of performance science, preventative healthcare, and personalized wellness.




JOB DESCRIPTION

Seeking a highly motivated and results-driven Business Development Associate to join our team. The ideal candidate will actively seek out and engage prospective customers to meet or exceed set quotas and goals, focusing on the Medical, Academic, Health/Wellness, or Fitness industries.

Please note: prior sales experience in the healthcare, medical, wellness or fitness sector is a requirement for this role.

This individual will play a key role in building strong customer relationships and aligning client priorities with the company's values. It is crucial that the candidate thrives in our entrepreneurial company culture where change is constant, growth is immense, and opportunities abound.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Demonstrate, promote and sell company products; strategically present functionality and key value propositions to prospective customers to meet and exceed sales quota.

 - Develop and foster relationships; maintain close communications with existing and prospective clients to close sales and promote customer retention.

 - Execute sales strategies; adhere to current and any updated strategies, KPI’s and policies.

 - Meet and exceed targets; achieve monthly, quarterly, and annual individual and team sales quota and goals for new customer acquisition.

 - Track progress and results; record prospect interactions and track goal attainment in CRM system.

 - Research and understand target market; stay abreast of industry trends, best practices, and the company's overall market opportunity.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor’s Degree or 1-3 years of relevant work experience.

 - Prior sales experience in the healthcare, fitness, wellness or medical industry is required

 - Strong ability to handle rejection and maintain high call/ meeting volume daily.
   Proficiency with CRM software (preferably HubSpot).

 - Demonstrated success in identifying and closing new business using a consultative, relationship-driven approach.

 - Excellent organizational and time management skills; ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines.

 - Outstanding oral and written communication skills, including persuasive negotiation and effective presentations.

 - Strong critical thinking, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.

 - Willingness to travel (at least 45 days per year) for business trips and industry tradeshows.

source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a smart seating company founded by ex-SpaceX and NASA engineers, building dynamic, sensor-driven medical cushions that prevent pressure injuries and restore mobility for wheelchair users.




ROLE OVERVIEW

Hiring a Head of Engineering to lead its entire engineering organization. This is the technical leader who turns a team of exceptionally strong individual contributors into a focused, fast-shipping unit — the glue between leadership's vision and the realities of the lab and the production floor.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead and hold accountable the entire engineering team — delivery, culture, morale, and accountability are all theirs

 - Bridge between leadership and the dev team — strategy gets communicated down, technical constraints get communicated up

 - Partner with the CEO and patent attorneys to actively draft and prosecute patents — this is a hands-on responsibility, not a hand-off

 - Hire and lead the engineer who will build our cloud connectivity and data platform

 - Collaborate closely with operations to ensure engineering decisions support our high-mix, short lead time manufacturing model

 - Ensure Sales & Marketing are kept abreast of timing, trade-offs, and impact of asks

 - Work with Finance on key programs for financial clarity




LEADERSHIP & CULTURE

 - Proven track record of adapting team culture as a company scales — maintaining morale and ensuring accountability through growth.

 - Builds teams that understand the customer deeply: engineers who talk to users and build with empathy.

 - Breaks down silos between engineering and the rest of the business.

 - Holds people accountable with clarity and respect — action items get closed, commitments get kept.

 - Holds strong opinions loosely and changes their mind when evidence warrants it.

 - Knows when to build vs. buy — allergic to reinventing the wheel.

 - Customer-obsessed: passionate about each customer's experience, prioritizing the real need and iterating against it.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years engineering experience with 3–5+ years leading engineering teams at a startup or growth-stage company

 - Hands-on background in electromechanical systems, embedded firmware, and connected hardware

 - Experience taking a hardware product and building the cloud connectivity and data platform around it

 - Proven track record of shipping complex hardware/software products on time and to spec

 - HIPAA or regulated data environment experience a plus

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a leading consumer Health & Wellness Platform — that has existing partnerships with Eight Sleep, WHOOP, Momentous, Peloton, and other top brands.




JOB DESCRIPTION

The primary objective of this role is to ensure that our marquee interventional health brand partners such as Peloton, ARMRA, and Sleep Number are not only successfully onboarded onto the platform but also positioned for long-term growth. Onboarding involves setting up these merchants to seamlessly accept HSA and FSA dollars from customers, ensuring a smooth and efficient implementation process. However, success does not stop at activation. This role is equally focused on maximizing revenue and engagement by helping partners fully leverage our capabilities over time.

To achieve this, the role works closely with our growth marketing team to develop co-marketing initiatives that drive customer awareness and adoption of HSA and FSA payment options. It also involves creating data-driven campaigns designed to boost transaction volume and merchant ROI while continuously analyzing performance to identify opportunities for optimization. This cross-functional partnership ensures that each merchant’s success is not only launched effectively but is also sustained and scaled through targeted marketing, ongoing analytics, and strategic collaboration.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Successfully Onboard New Merchants: The role begins with successfully onboarding new merchants by leading the end-to-end implementation for high-impact merchant partners. This includes ensuring that technical integration and marketing activation are seamless while setting each partner up for long-term success through a strategic onboarding process. By collaborating closely with the growth marketing team, the role helps design and launch campaigns that maximize awareness and adoption of our offerings from day one.

 - Be Merchant Obessed: Once onboarded, the focus shifts to being fully merchant-obsessed, providing white-glove service to our most impactful customers. This means continuously educating merchants on best practices, gathering feedback to inform both product and marketing improvements, and building deep, trust-based relationships that drive value and long-term loyalty. A strong partnership with growth marketing ensures that merchants receive ongoing campaign support and optimization to keep driving engagement and performance.

 - Drive Revenue Growth: Finally, the role plays a critical part in driving revenue growth by proactively identifying and executing creative, revenue-generating opportunities across the merchant portfolio. It involves managing multiple high-priority accounts simultaneously, balancing growth strategy, marketing initiatives, and operational execution. Acting as a trusted advisor, the role ensures not only smooth implementation but also sustained business success through close alignment with growth marketing and cross-functional teams.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Exhibits autonomy and maturity with a strong eagerness to create the best possible merchant experience in health and wellness

 - No strict education or experience requirements, though a demonstrated history of successful execution is strongly preferred

 - Backgrounds that could be a strong fit include early-stage startup executives, business development professionals, sales operations leaders, consultants, or individuals with finance experience

 - Proven ability to work cross-functionally and communicate effectively with both internal teams and external partners

 - Strong analytical skills and comfort with data-driven decision-making

 - Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and thrive in a fast-paced environment

 - Experience in growth marketing, partner success, or strategic account management is a plus

 - Familiarity with health, wellness, or fintech industr

source_jobs.title: PR & Communications Director, Americas
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a global brand at the forefront of longevity and health optimization, redefining how people approach performance, wellbeing, and long-term health.




ROLE OVERVIEW

The PR & Communications Director, Americas will be responsible for building, elevating and protecting the company’s brand reputation across the U.S. and the broader Americas region, with a strong focus on the U.S. as a strategic growth market.

This is a high-impact, externally facing leadership role, designed for a well-connected communications expert with a proven track record in premium/luxury brands, ideally within health, wellness, longevity, or lifestyle sectors.

This role will be instrumental in shaping the company’s perception as a leading global authority in health optimization and longevity.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


1. PR STRATEGY & BRAND POSITIONING

 - Define and execute the PR & Communications strategy for the Americas, aligned with global brand objectives

 - Position the company as a category-defining leader in longevity and proactive health

 - Lead storytelling across key verticals:
   
   - Longevity & preventive medicine
   
   - Executive health & performance
   
   - Women’s health & hormonal optimization
   
   - Regenerative therapies & advanced diagnostics
   
   - Lifestyle transformation & healthspan


2. MEDIA RELATIONS & EARNED MEDIA

 - Own and grow relationships with top-tier media outlets

 - Secure high-impact coverage, features, profiles, and expert placements

 - Actively manage media pipeline, ensuring consistent top-tier visibility


3. NETWORK, INFLUENCERS & THOUGHT LEADERS

 - Leverage and expand a strong network of:
   
   - Editors, journalists, and producers
   
   - Top-tier podcast hosts and platforms
   
   - KOLs in health, longevity, biohacking, performance, and luxury lifestyle

 - Identify and activate ambassadors and credible voices aligned with the company’s positioning

 - Drive participation of internal experts in high-authority conversations and platforms


4. PARTNERSHIPS & BRAND COLLABORATIONS

 - Develop strategic partnerships with:
   
   - Leading health & wellness brands
   
   - Longevity platforms and communities
   
   - Premium/luxury lifestyle brands
   
   - Corporate and executive networks

 - Structure collaborations that generate:
   
   - Visibility
   
   - Credibility
   
   - Qualified demand


5. EVENTS, EXPERIENCES & MARKET ACTIVATION

 - Lead PR-driven events across key feeder cities (NYC, Miami, LA, Mexico City, Vancouver, Toronto, etc.)
   
   - Media luncheons and press events
   
   - Thought leadership panels
   
   - Scientific and longevity-focused activations

 - Support flagship global moments

 - Ensure presence in relevant high-level ecosystems (private clubs, curated communities, industry events)


6. EXECUTIVE & MEDICAL THOUGHT LEADERSHIP

 - Position founders, executives, and medical experts as:
   
   - Global authorities in health optimization and longevity

 - Secure speaking opportunities, interviews, and editorial placements

 - Shape narratives that reinforce scientific credibility and aspirational lifestyle positioning


7. CRISIS & REPUTATION MANAGEMENT

 - Act as guardian of brand reputation in the region

 - Develop and implement crisis communication frameworks

 - Ensure message consistency and alignment with global standards


8. PERFORMANCE & REPORTING

 - Define and track PR KPIs:
   
   - Tier 1 media coverage
   
   - Share of voice
   
   - Brand sentiment
   
   - Audience reach and quality
   
   - Provide monthly and quarterly reporting to leadership




CANDIDATE PROFILE


EXPERIENCE

 - 10+ years in PR, Communications, or Brand Communications leadership roles

 - Proven experience in:
   
   - Luxury hospitality, wellness, health, or lifestyle brands
   
   - Preferably proactive health, longevity, or performance-driven br

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing and Growth
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client, the world’s largest direct-to-consumer genomics platform with the mission to power the future of personalized health by making whole genome sequencing and interpretation accessible, actionable, and meaningful for everyone.

 

Role Overview

The first Head of Marketing is a key member of the leadership team, accountable for scalable customer acquisition, strong retention, and disciplined, high-velocity revenue growth. This leader owns the end-to-end growth strategy across paid acquisition, lifecycle marketing, product marketing, and brand positioning. The ideal candidate has a proven record of scaling consumer or subscription businesses at 100%+ annual growth while tightly managing CAC, LTV, and contribution margin. They are both performance-driven and strategic, able to optimize acquisition at scale while building a differentiated, trusted brand in a complex category.

 

This role sits at the intersection of performance marketing, subscription economics, product storytelling, and category leadership. The Head of Marketing will build and lead a high-performing, multidisciplinary team that scales revenue efficiently and sustainably while reinforcing our position as the trusted leader in consumer genomics and preventative health.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the full growth funnel from acquisition through retention and expansion, with clear revenue and unit economics accountability.

 - Develop and execute an integrated growth strategy across paid media, lifecycle, product marketing, and brand.

 - Build and optimize a diversified paid acquisition mix, including Meta, Google, affiliates, and emerging channels.

 - Improve CAC, payback period, and contribution margin through structured experimentation.

 - Design and run A/B tests across creative, targeting, landing pages, and funnel flows

 - Lead onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention programs to grow subscription conversion and lifetime value.

 - Build lifecycle journeys across email, SMS, in-product messaging, and personalization to reduce churn.

 - Define and evolve the core value proposition and messaging architecture for the subscription offering.

 - Ensure alignment between acquisition messaging, product experience, and lifecycle communications.

 - Advance our brand toward category authority in personalized genomics while supporting performance goals.

 - Champion a data-driven, experimentation-first culture with clear testing roadmaps and impact-based prioritization.

 - Implement scalable growth processes and infrastructure that enable rapid testing and learning.

 - Build, mentor, and manage a high-performing marketing team across paid, lifecycle, product, and creative, operating both strategically and hands-on.

 


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 10+ years in growth or marketing leadership, ideally in digital health, biotech, or other regulated or high-trust categories.

 - Proven success scaling DTC subscription or recurring revenue businesses at 100%+ annual growth while managing CAC, LTV, and contribution margin.

 - Deep experience running multi-million-dollar paid acquisition programs with clear ownership of unit economics.

 - Strong background in lifecycle and subscription optimization, including onboarding, retention, and churn reduction.

 - Experience evolving brand positioning while scaling performance channels in competitive markets.

 - Hands-on expertise designing and running structured experimentation and A/B testing across the funnel.

 - Track record building and leading cross-functional marketing teams across growth, lifecycle, product marketing, and creative.

 - Experience implementing marketing systems and processes in a fast-growing startup environment.

source_jobs.title: Affiliate Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a leading consumer Health & Wellness Platform — that has existing partnerships with Eight Sleep, WHOOP, Momentous, Peloton, and other top brands.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We are hiring an Affiliate Marketing Manager to own our affiliate program — a distinct, opt-in merchant program that complements the merchant experience.

This is an operator role inside Marketing. You'll own the program's economics, platform operations across partner affiliate networks (Impact, UpPromote, CJ, Everflow), program reporting (revenue, enrollment, deliverable fulfillment), and the value narrative that positions the affiliate program to prospective members.

You'll work closely with our Partner Marketing, Lifecycle Marketing, Sales, and Merchant Success teams, who execute the deliverables and run the commercial conversations this role supports.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Program Operations
   
   - Own Affiliate Program end-to-end: membership operations, tracking setup, commission structure, payout operations, and ongoing program management across multiple affiliate networks.
   
   - Define and maintain the program's commission structure and member benefits — what affiliate members pay, what they receive, and the economics that make it work for both the merchant and us.
   
   - Partner with Finance to structure payment flows, invoicing, and revenue recognition for affiliate-sourced transactions across all networks.

 - Deliverables Fulfillment
   
   - Maintain and evolve the standard set of deliverables offered at each enrollment level.
   
   - Serve as the single point of truth for each affiliate member's deliverable schedule — briefing affiliate-specific asks into Partner Marketing, Lifecycle Marketing, and other execution teams, and confirming everything lands.
   
   - Maintain the affiliate program's marketing inventory: the full list of deliverables available to members, their quality standards, and their capacity constraints.

 - Program Growth & Reporting
   
   - Build and maintain the value story and ROI case for the affiliate program, both internally and externally.
   
   - Act as the affiliate program's subject matter expert in merchant conversations — presenting inventory, explaining economics, and walking merchants through what they unlock by opting in.
   
   - Deliver quarterly affiliate-member reporting so every member sees the data that justifies their investment.
   
   - Own program-level reporting: member count, enrollment pipeline, affiliate-attributed revenue, deliverable fulfillment rate, and churn.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - Operationally rigorous — you build systems, playbooks, and calendars that keep a multi-platform, multi-member program clean.

 - Data-driven — you lead with numbers and can translate transaction data into compelling value stories, program economics, and member reporting.

 - A self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and is comfortable building a program from scratch.

 - An excellent communicator and subject matter expert — you can present a program's inventory and value proposition directly to merchant marketing teams without it becoming a sales pitch.

 - Energized by cross-functional collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager - Supplements
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a innovative gut health supplement company.




JOB DESCRIPTION

The Product Lead is responsible for owning products end-to-end — from opportunity identification through development, launch, and in-market optimization.

This role serves as the single accountable owner of each product, working cross-functionally with formulation, supply chain, regulatory, quality, brand, and customer experience to ensure products are scientifically credible, operationally feasible, commercially successful, and compliant across markets.

Owns everything that happens to a SKU after formulation is locked — from artwork to market entry to post-market surveillance — across every country we sell in.





RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Packaging and artwork execution (dielines, label copy, market variants)

 - Regulatory approvals and market entry across US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia and New Zealand

 - Pursuing and maintaining certifications (NSF Certified, FODMAP Friendly, Glyphosate Free etc.)

 - Testing program: third-party testing, NSF testing, stability and shelf-life

 - Label claims and claims substantiation, including change control when anything upstream shifts

 - Post-market surveillance — complaint trending, adverse event reporting, recall readiness

 - Coordinating scientific reviews for marketing, CX, and other internal teams

 - Supporting research studies




REQUIREMENTS

 - 6–10+ years of experience in supplements, functional CPG, or a related consumer product category

 - Demonstrated experience owning products end-to-end, including post-launch management

 - Comfortable managing external experts (formulators, labs, consultants)

 - Strong judgment, prioritization, and communication skills

 - Solid understanding of cost structures, margins, and operational constraints

 - Thrives in fast-growing, founder-led environments

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client building a consumer health platform that measures and interprets hormone and biomarker data, translating complex physiological insights into personalized guidance through a mobile app designed to help people better understand and take action on their health.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Lead performance marketing and growth for a consumer health platform.

This role sits at the intersection of analytical rigor and creative experimentation. You’ll own the performance marketing strategy and manage a meaningful paid acquisition budget across established and emerging channels.

You’ll be responsible for building the growth operating model, scaling paid acquisition programs, and continuously testing creative and channel strategies that drive efficient user growth.

This role works closely with product, finance, and lifecycle marketing teams to ensure acquisition strategies align with product experience, retention, and overall unit economics.

Montreal-based preferred.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Growth Strategy & Operations

 - Build and maintain the growth operating model, including forecasting, target setting, and capital allocation across acquisition initiatives

 - Own performance marketing spend across Meta (Facebook, Instagram), Google (Search, YouTube), and emerging channels such as TikTok and Reddit

 - Partner with finance and marketing leadership on media budgets, investment decisions, and growth forecasting

 - Develop measurement and incrementality frameworks that accurately capture performance impact

Performance Marketing Execution

 - Manage day-to-day paid acquisition performance across multiple channels, ensuring campaigns hit payback period and efficiency targets

 - Develop and test ad creative and messaging that improves conversion rates and acquisition costs

 - Launch and scale new acquisition channels, moving quickly from experimentation to meaningful contribution

 - Partner with agencies and internal teams to optimize campaign performance and creative production

 - Leverage AI-powered tools and analytics platforms to improve campaign performance and workflow efficiency

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with product teams to ensure acquisition strategies align with the product roadmap and user experience

 - Work with lifecycle marketing to optimize the full funnel from acquisition through retention

 - Contribute to go-to-market and growth strategies, including incentives, onboarding experiences, and renewal mechanics

 - Run structured experiments and share insights across the organization

Analytics & Forecasting

 - Analyze marginal ROI across channels to inform budget allocation

 - Forecast performance gaps and develop strategies to close them

 - Monitor key unit economics metrics, including CAC, LTV, and payback periods

 - Build dashboards and reporting that make performance data accessible to stakeholders




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4–6 years of performance marketing or user acquisition experience managing significant marketing budgets ($1M+ annually)

 - Proven track record of scaling paid acquisition while maintaining strong unit economics

 - Deep expertise in Meta and Google advertising platforms, with experience testing emerging channels

 - Strong analytical skills with proficiency in SQL, Excel/Google Sheets, and analytics platforms

 - Experience developing and testing high-performing ad creative

 - Familiarity with AI-powered marketing and creative tools that improve workflow efficiency and experimentation

 - Ability to operate in a fast-moving environment with high ownership and accountability

 - Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with product, marketing, and analytics teams




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in health tech, wellness, or consumer subscription businesses

 - Track record of helping scale a new product or business unit

 - E

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client, a premier West Coast active lifestyle company, that blends resort-style amenities, family programming, and community into a single shared membership where members can do it all.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Seeking a highly organized, proactive, and polished Executive Assistant to support senior leadership, specifically our Chief People and Experience Officer and Chief Financial Officer (CFO) in a fast-paced and highly collaborative environment.

 

This role requires exceptional attention to detail, strong communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities with professionalism and discretion. The Executive Assistant will serve as a trusted partner to executives, helping drive efficiency, organization, communication, and execution across daily operations and




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage complex calendars, scheduling, travel coordination, and meeting logistics.

 - Coordinate internal and external communications on behalf of executive leadership.

 - Prepare presentations, reports, agendas, and meeting materials.

 - Support cross-functional projects and help manage follow-up on key initiatives.

 - Organize team events, leadership meetings, and executive offsites.

 - Maintain confidentiality and professionalism while handling sensitive information.

 - Serve as a liaison between executives and internal/external stakeholders.

 - Anticipate needs and proactively solve problems in a dynamic environment.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of executive support experience supporting senior-level leaders.

 - Ability to operate effectively in a fast-moving, high-growth organization.

 - Exceptional organizational and time-management skills.

 - Strong written and verbal communication abilities.

 - High degree of professionalism, discretion, and attention to detail.

 - Advanced proficiency with Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and scheduling tools.

 - Demonstrates proficiency in leveraging AI tools and technologies to streamline workflows, enhance productivity, support executive decision-making, and improve operational efficiency.

source_jobs.title: Supply Chain and Logistics Lead
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client building a consumer health platform that measures and interprets hormone and biomarker data, translating complex physiological insights into personalized guidance through a mobile app designed to help people better understand and take action on their health.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We ship a physical product across borders and are scaling fast. We are looking for a Supply Chain and Fulfillment Lead to own our supply chain, procurement, logistics, and fulfillment operations end-to-end. Today that means Canada-to-US shipping with customs complexity, multiple suppliers, a growing order volume, and a fulfillment cost structure that needs to improve as we scale.

You will own everything between our suppliers and our customers’ doorsteps: sourcing, procurement, inventory, shipping, and order fulfillment.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Supply chain and procurement

 - Build a diversified supply chain with backup suppliers for all critical components. No single-source dependencies.

 - Reduce COGS through vendor negotiations, alternative sourcing, and process improvements.

 - Own vendor selection, qualification, audits, and long-term relationship management.

 - Manage vendor risk. Know what breaks if a supplier goes down, and have a plan.

Logistics and fulfilment

 - Eliminate current cross-border delays and customs friction for US customers.

 - Stand up end-to-end order management from purchase to doorstep.

 - Manage 3PL and carrier relationships with clear performance SLAs.

 - Reduce per-order fulfillment cost below the current $10+ baseline as volume scales.

Inventory and systems

 - Roll out inventory tracking and procurement planning systems that are fit for scale.

 - Build operational reporting that gives leadership real-time visibility into inventory levels, landed costs, and order status.

 - Own the interface between supply chain and manufacturing on materials planning and production scheduling.

Partners and vendors

 - Set performance standards and accountability across external partners: suppliers, freight forwarders, 3PLs, carriers.

 - Reduce founder dependency on day-to-day supply chain and shipping decisions.

 - This description is not exhaustive. We are looking for someone who has built supply chain and fulfillment operations from scratch, not someone who has only managed inherited structures.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - You have owned supply chain, procurement, and fulfillment end-to-end at a physical-product company. Not as a contributor on a large team. As the person responsible.

 - You have scaled fulfillment from hundreds to thousands of orders per month, ideally at a Series A–C startup with a team under 50.

 - You have managed cross-border shipping between Canada and the US or comparable complexity, including customs, duties, FDA requirements, carrier selection and importer-of-record structures.

 - You have diversified supplier bases and reduced COGS in a resource-constrained environment.

 - You have a background in consumer health, diagnostics, medical devices, or food tech. You understand regulated product shipping and import/export requirements.

 - You operate at both altitude and ground level. You can negotiate a freight contract in the morning and troubleshoot a stuck shipment in the afternoon.

 - You bring clarity and discipline to chaotic, fast-moving contexts.

 - You make decisions independently and hold vendors to account.

 - You work closely with manufacturing without needing to own it. You understand BOMs, production schedules, and materials planning well enough to be an effective partner to the production team.

source_jobs.title: Vice President - General Manager
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm working with top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client, a premier West Coast active lifestyle company, that blends resort-style amenities, family programming, and community into a single shared membership where members can do it all.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Vice President / General Manager serves as the senior leader for a campus or regional portfolio, responsible for delivering exceptional member experiences, operational excellence, team leadership, and financial performance. This role is critical in driving our sports, outdoor recreation, and active lifestyle-forward culture while building strong community engagement and maximizing long-term member loyalty.

 

The VP/GM leads cross-functional club operations including fitness, sports, food & beverage, aquatics, family programming, hospitality, facilities, and member engagement. This leader acts as both a strategic operator and culture builder, creating an environment where team members thrive and members feel deeply connected to our community.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead all aspects of club and campus operations with accountability for revenue growth, profitability, member satisfaction, and team engagement.

 - Drive membership growth, retention, and utilization through elevated hospitality and programming experiences.

 - Ensure operational excellence across safety, cleanliness, service standards, and facilities management.

 - Build and develop high-performing leadership teams across operations, fitness, hospitality, and programming.

 - Champion our culture and ensure alignment with the company’s Code of Culture.

 - Partner cross-functionally with corporate leadership teams including operations, marketing, finance, people operations, and member experience.

 - Create a strong community presence through events, and member engagement initiatives.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 10+ years of leadership experience in active lifestyle, hospitality, fitness, resorts, private clubs, or multi-unit operations.

 - Strong financial acumen with experience managing complex P&Ls.

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment.

 - Proven success leading large operational teams in customer-centric environments.

 - Exceptional leadership presence with the ability to inspire, coach, and develop teams.

 - Passion for sports, outdoor recreation, active lifestyle, wellness, recreation, and community-building.

source_jobs.title: Director of Global Sales - Luxury Hospitality
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.



We’re filling this role for a client, a leading brand for healthspan optimization and integrative longevity medicine.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We are looking for a hands-on, front-line commercial leader. This is a player-coach role: you will personally open doors, build relationships, and close high-value corporate and UHNW business from day one while building and leading a high performance team, channels, and systems that scale that production across markets.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Personal Selling and Network Activation
   
   - Own a top-tier book of named accounts and UHNW relationships. Personally open, advance, and close them.
   
   - Activate your existing network from week one to generate qualified pipeline and confirmed bookings.
   
   - Be most credible commercial face in the room with C-suite leaders, family offices, and UHNW clients.

 - Commercial Leadership and P&L
   
   - Hold commercial P&L accountability for top line performance across properties.
   
   - Translate the defined commercial strategy into a clear, time-bound execution plan governed by weekly numbers.
   
   - Lead from the front: model the selling behavior and intensity you expect from the team.

 - Build and Lead High-Performance Sales Teams
   
   - Recruit, structure, and lead hunter-profile sales teams in key markets (Europe, North America, Middle East, Latin America, Asia), close to target clients.
   
   - Implement an aggressive, performance-based variable compensation plan tied to revenue, conversion, and new-business acquisition.
   
   - Run rigorous accountability: weekly pipeline reviews, revenue per salesperson, conversion by channel, transparent KPI dashboards.
   
   - Build a demanding, high-standards culture of relentless prospecting and cross-selling across verticals.

 - Commercial Verticals
   
   Lead high-impact revenue verticals, each with a tailored go-to-market and dedicated director- level leadership:
   
   - Trade & Leisure — luxury travel advisors, consortia, OTAs, and high-end tour operators —
     
     optimal channel mix with rate integrity.
   
   - Corporate / Executive Health — C-suite retreats, incentive travel, and organizational health programs.
   
   - Commercial Partnerships —private membership networks, YPO/EO/Vistage, private aviation, longevity and active living brands, wealth management.
   
   - Healthcare Ambassadors & Institutions — referral agreements with hospitals, clinics, precision-medicine practices, and KOLs, tracked in CRM.
   
   - Member-Get-Member / UHNW Ambassadors — an elite referral program driving qualified leads and repeat stays.
   
   - Sports & High-Performance — professional athletes, federations, and performance coaches
   
   


IDEAL PROFILE

 - Direct sales experience in health, wellness, longevity, or medical / healthcare — you know this client and this product.

 - A solid, portable, activatable network across UHNW individuals, corporate C-suite, family offices, luxury travel trade, and/or healthcare referrers that you can switch on immediately — a true plug-and-play hire.

 - A proven personal closing record — you have personally carried and hit a senior revenue number, not only managed others who did.

 - Solid revenue-management and pricing experience — yield, channel mix, rate integrity.

 - Operator / doer mentality — hands-on, dynamic, high-energy; leads by example in the field and on the phones.

 - Willing and energized to travel frequently and internationally.

 - Proven experience managing and leading a contact center / inside-sales team — hands-on ownership of scripts, routing, speed-to-lead, coaching, and conversion, with a track record of turning it into measurable booked revenue.

 - Strong, demanding, accountable leadership with high standards.

source_jobs.title: VP of Growth Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Fitt Talent Partners is a specialized recruitment firm for top health and wellness companies.

We’re filling this role for a client, A real-food nutrition company focused on regeneratively raised meat snacks and supplements, crafted to make eating like a human easy, honest, and impactful.




JOB DESCRIPTION

We’re hiring a VP of Growth to own the entire growth engine. This is not a channel role. This is not a “manage agencies” role. This is a build, lead, and scale the machine role.

You will architect and run a modern DTC growth system across:

 - Paid acquisition

 - Organic / founder-led growth

 - Creative strategy

 - Affiliate + partnerships

 - Lifecycle + retention

 - Offer + funnel design

You’ll lead a cross-functional team and be directly responsible for driving new customer growth, CAC efficiency, and LTV expansion.





RESPONSIBILITIES



Growth Strategy & System Design

 - Build and continuously evolve our growth playbooks

 - Design scalable systems for:
   
   - Paid acquisition (Meta-first, multi-channel expansion)
   
   - UGC + creator pipelines
   
   - Affiliate / performance partnerships
   
   - Founder-led organic growth (critical)

 - Translate strategy into clear weekly execution + KPIs

Paid Acquisition at Scale

 - Oversee multi-million dollar annual spend

 - Drive performance across: Meta (core), Google / YouTube, Emerging channels (TikTok, etc.)

 - Optimize to contribution, not vanity ROAS

 - Continuously push new audience + creative expansion

Creative Strategy Leadership

 - Own the creative engine, not just performance

 - Build repeatable systems for:
   
   - High-volume UGC production
   
   - Ad concept pipelines (hooks, angles, offers)
   
   - Iteration velocity

 - Partner tightly with content + founder to unlock scroll-stopping creative

Founder-Led Growth Flywheel

 - Build systems that convert: Organic reach → leads → customers

 - Launch and scale:
   
   - Lead magnets
   
   - Advertorial funnels
   
   - “Blog ads” / whitelisting strategies

Offers, Funnels & Monetization

 - Constantly ideate and test:
   
   - Offers (bundles, discounts, entry points)
   
   - Funnels (sample packs, subscriptions, lead gen)

 - Improve AOV, Conversion rate, and New customer acquisition efficiency

Retention & LTV Expansion

 - Build a subscription-first growth engine

 - Drive Repeat purchase rate and Cohort LTV (30 / 60 / 90 day)

 - Partner with Retention lead on:
   
   - Email / SMS
   
   - Post-purchase flows
   
   - Retention campaigns

Team Leadership

 - Lead and develop a high-performance growth org:
   
   - Paid, Retention, Creative / Content, Affiliates, and Brand / Partnerships

 - Set clear ownership, KPIs, quarterly rocks and accountability

 - Create a culture of Speed, Ownership, Relentless iteration




ABOUT YOU

 - 6+ years of DTC eCommerce Growth experience (CPG / VMS highly preferred)

 - Expert understanding of building repeatable, scalable growth systems, not just paid ads

 - Team leader and people manager, capable of driving channel-level performance

 - Expert knowledge of how to efficiently scale new customer acquisition for a subscription-heavy CPG brand

 - World-class creative strategist: can build frameworks for ad creatives, angles, hooks, messaging, understand landing page CRO best practices, etc.

 - Shopify ecosystem expert (Platform functionality, apps, integrations)

 - Expert knowledge of advanced DTC marketing metrics (nCAC, aMER, iROAS, etc)

 - Experience leveraging next-gen attribution tactics / platforms for incrementality testing / MMM modeling (Haus, Prescient, Triple Whale, Northbeam)

 - Very strong retention knowledge, especially around driving subscriber LTV / churn reduction

 - Deeply analytical and comfortable modeling first-order profitability and ROAS vs LTV

 - Ability to design and test landing pages, page components and UX/UI enhancements as part of an ongoing CRO process

 - Can manage external agencies and contractors while owni

notes: Health and wellness companies, various roles, no explicit sponsorship or clearance mentions
